file.content:
#: Terminal conclusions. A component may not state these — the reducer derives them.
#:
#: Sol V9 §1, verbatim: "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK, VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE,
#: or an expression edge. Those are reducer outputs derived from immutable metadata and document bytes."
#: FULLTEXT, ADMISSIBLE and `same work` were already here. THIS_WORK and VERSION_OF_RECORD were not —
#: and an adapter that may write `version_of_record` into a payload has been handed, by the back door,
#: precisely the authority the V9 P0 was about taking away from it. A repository saying `acceptedVersion`
#: is an OBSERVATION; a component writing `version_of_record` is a VERDICT.
RESERVED_VALUES = (
    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
    'complete', 'route_complete',
    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
    'same work', 'same_work', 'this work', 'this_work', 'different work', 'different_work',
    'version equivalent', 'version of record', 'version_of_record',
    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
)

#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
RESERVED_KEYS = (
    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
)

#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words
#: inside their paths (`/articles/PMC.../?report=fulltext`, `/science/article/.../fulltext`), and a
#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the
#: string "fulltext" — would have made the locator unrecordable and left us exactly where we were:
#: holding an NBER working paper under a URL pointing at aeaweb.org.
#: `requested_venue` sits beside `requested_title` for the same reason: it is a NAME, quoted from the
#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the
#: journal's name contains one of our reserved words, would be a guard against nothing.
VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
})


def _scan_value(k: str, v, bad: list[str], path: str = '') -> None:
    """Scan ONE value, RECURSIVELY.

    IT DID NOT RECURSE. The scan ran over `payload.items()` and tested `isinstance(v, str)` — so a
    conclusion one level down was invisible to it:

        emit(..., adapter_observations={'content_status': 'FULLTEXT'})    # ACCEPTED. Silently.

    A guard that only inspects the top level of a nested structure is a guard you step over by adding
    a dict, and "the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses" is the sentence this whole
    project is trying to stop writing. Payloads stay flat by convention; this makes it true by force.
    """
    where = f'{path}{k}'
    if isinstance(v, str):
        vl = v.lower().strip()
        for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
            # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
            if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
                bad.append(f'{where}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
                           f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
                return
    elif isinstance(v, dict):
        for k2, v2 in v.items():
            if str(k2).lower() in RESERVED_KEYS:
                bad.append(f'key {where}.{k2!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may '
                           f'not set it, and burying it one level down does not make it an observation')
                continue
            if str(k2).lower() in VERBATIM_KEYS:
                continue
            _scan_value(str(k2), v2, bad, path=f'{where}.')
    elif isinstance(v, (list, tuple)):
        for i, v2 in enumerate(v):
            _scan_value(f'[{i}]', v2, bad, path=where)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Event:
    """An OBSERVATION. Immutable, ordered, and never a conclusion."""
    seq: int
    unit: str                    # what this is about — a DOI, a coverage cell id, a route id
    kind: str
    actor: str                   # which component observed it (for provenance, not for authority)
    payload: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    ts: float = field(default_factory=time.time)

    def to_json(self) -> str:
        return json.dumps({'seq': self.seq, 'unit': self.unit, 'kind': self.kind,
                           'actor': self.actor, 'payload': self.payload, 'ts': self.ts},
                          sort_keys=True)

content.text: Confirmed root cause: `observe_text` emits `article_front_matter` (raw document text, line 590) but the guard's `VERBATIM_KEYS` exemption list omits it, so any article whose front matter contains a reserved word fails to record. Let me see all world-text fields `observe_text` emits.

content.content:
534	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
535	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
536	
537	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
538	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
539	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
540	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
541	    """
542	    words = text.split()
543	    header = text[:header_chars]
544	    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
545	    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
546	    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
547	    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
548	    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
549	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
550	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
551	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
552	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
553	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
554	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
555	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
556	    return {
557	        'word_count': len(words),
558	        'char_count': len(text),
559	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
560	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
561	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
562	        # ---- VERSION FURNITURE, READ IN THE RIGHT VOICE ----------------------------------------
563	        # A stamp means something different depending on WHO PRINTED IT. The article's own front
564	        # matter is the DOCUMENT'S TESTIMONY ABOUT ITSELF; the cover sheet is THE LIBRARY'S OPINION OF
565	        # WHAT IT FILED. These were previously read from the same undifferentiated `header` slice, so a
566	        # library's "citation for the published version" was scored as the article calling itself the
567	        # version of record. They are separated now, and `derive_semantic_binding` weighs them by voice.
568	        'has_cover_sheet': bool(cover),
569	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
570	        'published_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(cover)),
571	        'accepted_stamp_in_header': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
572	        'accepted_stamp_in_cover': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(cover)),
573	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(article_front)),
574	        'preprint_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(cover)),
575	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
576	        'header_real_words': header_real,
577	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
578	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
579	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
580	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
581	        'identity_window': header,
582	        'header_text': header[:400],
583	        # ---- SOL V9 §5: IDENTITY FROM THE FETCHED CONTENT ITSELF -------------------------------
584	        # The DOI the ARTICLE prints on itself (JATS article-DOI, PDF front matter, HTML header) — with
585	        # any repository COVER SHEET segmented off, because a cover sheet's DOI is the library's opinion
586	        # of what it deposited, not the document's testimony about what it is.
587	        'front_matter_dois': front_dois,
588	        'cover_sheet_chars': len(cover),
589	        'cover_sheet_text': cover[:400],
590	        'article_front_matter': article_front[:400],
591	        # A BYLINE IS PRESENT — or it is not. `False` means the reducer may not reason about
592	        # disjointness AT ALL: an absent byline is not a disjoint one. (A cookie banner names no author
593	        # because it is not a document, not because a stranger wrote it.)
594	        'byline_present': bool(_BYLINE_CUE.search(article_front) or _BYLINE.search(article_front)),
595	    }
596

file.content:
def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.

    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
    """
    words = text.split()
    header = text[:header_chars]
    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
    return {
        'word_count': len(words),
        'char_count': len(text),
        'readable_word_count': real_words,
        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
        # ---- VERSION FURNITURE, READ IN THE RIGHT VOICE ----------------------------------------
        # A stamp means something different depending on WHO PRINTED IT. The article's own front
        # matter is the DOCUMENT'S TESTIMONY ABOUT ITSELF; the cover sheet is THE LIBRARY'S OPINION OF
        # WHAT IT FILED. These were previously read from the same undifferentiated `header` slice, so a
        # library's "citation for the published version" was scored as the article calling itself the
        # version of record. They are separated now, and `derive_semantic_binding` weighs them by voice.
        'has_cover_sheet': bool(cover),
        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
        'published_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(cover)),
        'accepted_stamp_in_header': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
        'accepted_stamp_in_cover': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(cover)),
        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(article_front)),
        'preprint_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(cover)),
        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
        'header_real_words': header_real,
        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
        'identity_window': header,
        'header_text': header[:400],
        # ---- SOL V9 §5: IDENTITY FROM THE FETCHED CONTENT ITSELF -------------------------------
        # The DOI the ARTICLE prints on itself (JATS article-DOI, PDF front matter, HTML header) — with
        # any repository COVER SHEET segmented off, because a cover sheet's DOI is the library's opinion
        # of what it deposited, not the document's testimony about what it is.
        'front_matter_dois': front_dois,
        'cover_sheet_chars': len(cover),
        'cover_sheet_text': cover[:400],
        'article_front_matter': article_front[:400],
        # A BYLINE IS PRESENT — or it is not. `False` means the reducer may not reason about
        # disjointness AT ALL: an absent byline is not a disjoint one. (A cookie banner names no author
        # because it is not a document, not because a stranger wrote it.)
        'byline_present': bool(_BYLINE_CUE.search(article_front) or _BYLINE.search(article_front)),
    }


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ---- 1. backend outcome ----------------------------------------------------------------------

RESPONDED            = 'RESPONDED'                    # the backend answered with content
NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST
ACCESS_BLOCKED       = 'ACCESS_BLOCKED'               # 401/403/paywall — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
NO_OUTCOME           = 'NO_OUTCOME'                   # attempted, never came back. A HANG, not a gap.

content.text: Root cause confirmed: `article_front_matter` and `cover_sheet_text` are raw document text (lines 589-590) but were never added to the guard's `VERBATIM_KEYS` world-text exemption — so any real article whose front matter contains a reserved word (e.g. "complete", "validated", "supported") fails to record. This is exactly the silent-drop failure mode to avoid. Let me check tests reference it, then apply the minimal intent-aligned fix.

#: `article_front_matter` and `cover_sheet_text` are the two halves segment_front_matter() carves out
#: of the fetched document (observe_text, below) — RAW DOCUMENT TEXT, in the same category as
#: `header_text` and `identity_window`. A real article's front matter routinely contains a reserved
#: word ("Complete list of authors" on a PubMed page, a paper whose title includes "validated" or
#: "supported"), and a guard that refused to record the document's own front matter because the
#: document contains one of our words would silently DROP the paper — the exact failure this project
#: exists to end. They are exempt from the VALUE scan (never from the key scan), like every other
#: quote from the world.
VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
    'article_front_matter', 'cover_sheet_text',
})

content.text: The run is healthy, but OpenAlex governor deferrals (RESOLVE_FAILED + DEFERRED ≈ 60% so far) are recoverable retriables — a single pass leaves yield on the table. Since this is the project's long pole, I'll add a bounded auto-loop so the detached process drains retriables across passes without manual re-runs, then relaunch.

pending = [d for d in ordered
               if done.get(d, {}).get('outcome') not in TERMINAL]     # retriable + unstarted
    if args.offset:
        pending = pending[args.offset:]
    if args.limit:
        pending = pending[:args.limit]
    print(f'    {len(pending)} DOIs to process this run '
          f'(terminal skipped; retriable re-attempted)\n', flush=True)

    for i, doi in enumerate(pending, 1):
        entry = cache.get(doi)
        try:
            rec = process_doi(acq, ledger, blobs, doi, entry)
        except Exception as e:
            rec = {'doi': doi, 'outcome': 'BACKEND_FAILED',
                   'detail': {'error': f'{type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:200]}'}, 'ts': time.time()}
        append_checkpoint(rec)
        done[norm_doi(doi)] = rec
        d = rec['detail']
        print(f"  [{i:>4}/{len(pending)}] {rec['outcome']:<14} "
              f"loc={d.get('n_locations', '?'):>2} got={d.get('n_fetched', 0)} "
              f"cls={d.get('content_class', '')} expr={d.get('expression_kind', '')} "
              f"words={d.get('readable_words', 0)} :: {doi}", flush=True)
        if i % 10 == 0 or i == len(pending):
            write_status(done, total, i)

    write_status(done, total, len(pending))
input.new_string:
# ── PASSES. One pass processes every pending DOI once. RETRIABLE outcomes (OpenAlex deferred by our
    #    own governor, a 429, a hang) are facts about OUR request rate, not the world — so we do more
    #    passes: between them the per-host `not_before` expires and the breaker clears, and a DOI that
    #    DEFERRED because OpenAlex was cooling down resolves cleanly next time. We stop when nothing is
    #    pending, or a whole pass converted ZERO retriables to terminal (no progress => waiting longer
    #    will not help this process), or MAX_PASSES is hit. `--limit` forces a single pass (test slice).
    max_passes = 1 if args.limit else int(os.environ.get('POLARIS_MAX_PASSES', '6'))
    for p in range(1, max_passes + 1):
        pending = [d for d in ordered
                   if done.get(d, {}).get('outcome') not in TERMINAL]   # retriable + unstarted
        if args.offset:
            pending = pending[args.offset:]
        if args.limit:
            pending = pending[:args.limit]
        if not pending:
            print(f'\n=== all DOIs terminal after pass {p - 1}. nothing retriable remains. ===',
                  flush=True)
            break
        n_terminal_before = sum(1 for r in done.values() if r.get('outcome') in TERMINAL)
        print(f'\n=== PASS {p}/{max_passes}: {len(pending)} DOIs pending '
              f'(terminal skipped; retriable re-attempted) ===\n', flush=True)

        for i, doi in enumerate(pending, 1):
            entry = cache.get(doi)
            try:
                rec = process_doi(acq, ledger, blobs, doi, entry)
            except Exception as e:
                rec = {'doi': doi, 'outcome': 'BACKEND_FAILED',
                       'detail': {'error': f'{type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:200]}'}, 'ts': time.time()}
            append_checkpoint(rec)
            done[norm_doi(doi)] = rec
            d = rec['detail']
            print(f"  p{p} [{i:>4}/{len(pending)}] {rec['outcome']:<14} "
                  f"loc={d.get('n_locations', '?'):>2} got={d.get('n_fetched', 0)} "
                  f"cls={d.get('content_class', '')} expr={d.get('expression_kind', '')} "
                  f"words={d.get('readable_words', 0)} :: {doi}", flush=True)
            if i % 10 == 0 or i == len(pending):
                write_status(done, total, i)

        n_terminal_after = sum(1 for r in done.values() if r.get('outcome') in TERMINAL)
        gained = n_terminal_after - n_terminal_before
        print(f'\n=== PASS {p} done: +{gained} DOIs reached a terminal outcome this pass ===',
              flush=True)
        if gained == 0:
            print('    no retriable DOI converted this pass — waiting longer will not help. Stopping.',
                  flush=True)
            break
        if p < max_passes:
            # let per-host not_before windows expire before hammering the governor again
            time.sleep(float(os.environ.get('POLARIS_PASS_COOLDOWN_S', '30')))

    write_status(done, total, total)

class Ledger:
    """Append-only, DURABLE, and RELOADABLE. You may add an observation. You may not edit, delete,
    or conclude.

    IT DID NOT RELOAD. `__init__` set `self._events = []` and never read the file, so a ledger opened
    over an existing JSONL began with an EMPTY HISTORY — while still appending to the end of it. Every
    standalone script therefore reduced over the handful of events IT had emitted, not over what
    happened. `derive_route_status()` would find one adapter planned instead of four and call the route
    COMPLETE; `derive_coverage_status()` would license a scoped absence off a history it could not see.
    A reducer over an append-only log that does not read the log is not a reducer over anything — and
    it fails in the direction of CONFIDENCE, which is the only direction that ships.
    """

    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None, *, load: bool = True):
        self._events: list[Event] = []
        self._path = Path(path) if path else None
        self._seq = 0
        if self._path is not None:
            self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            if load and self._path.exists():
                self._load()

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, path: Path | str) -> 'Ledger':
        """Open the ledger AS IT STANDS ON DISK. This is the constructor a standalone script wants."""
        return cls(path, load=True)

    # -- read the history back --------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _load(self) -> None:
        """A PERSISTED LEDGER IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT, and it is exactly as untrusted as the corpus was.

        The emit-time guard is RE-APPLIED here. Bypassing it on read would leave the door it locks
        standing open: `echo '{"payload":{"content_status":"FULLTEXT"}}' >> ledger.jsonl` is not a
        harder attack than calling emit(), it is an easier one.

        A record written by a reducer (it carries `derived_by`) is exempt from that scan, because its
        whole job is to hold a derived verdict. Forging one buys nothing: NO REDUCER READS THEM. They
        are audit artifacts, and every derive_*() in this file re-derives from the OBSERVATIONS. That
        is the difference between a ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped
        FULLTEXT on a cookie banner.
        """
        seen: set[int] = set()
        events: list[Event] = []
        for i, line in enumerate(self._path.read_text().splitlines(), 1):
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            where = f'{self._path.name}:{i}'
            try:
                d = json.loads(line)
            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: not JSON — {e}')
            missing = {'seq', 'unit', 'kind', 'actor', 'payload', 'ts'} - set(d)
            if missing:
                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
            if d['kind'] not in EventKind.ALL:
                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: unknown event kind {d["kind"]!r}')
            if not isinstance(d['payload'], dict):
                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: payload is not an object')
            if not isinstance(d['seq'], int) or d['seq'] in seen:
                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: seq {d["seq"]!r} is duplicated or not an integer — an '
                                    f'append-only log with a repeated sequence number has been edited')
            if 'derived_by' not in d['payload']:
                problems = _scan_for_conclusions(d['kind'], d['payload'])
                if problems:
                    raise LedgerCorrupt(
                        f'{where}: this line writes a CONCLUSION into the ledger — the emit guard '
                        f'refuses it, and so does the loader:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
            seen.add(d['seq'])
            events.append(Event(seq=d['seq'], unit=d['unit'], kind=d['kind'], actor=d['actor'],
                                payload=d['payload'], ts=d['ts']))
        events.sort(key=lambda e: e.seq)
        self._events = events
        self._seq = max(seen, default=0)     # ...so the next append CONTINUES the log, never collides

    # -- write side: exactly one method, and it is guarded -------------------------------------
    def emit(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, **payload) -> Event:
        if kind not in EventKind.ALL:
            raise ForbiddenLabel(f'unknown event kind {kind!r}')
        problems = _scan_for_conclusions(kind, payload)
        if problems:
            raise ForbiddenLabel(
                f'{actor} tried to write a CONCLUSION into the ledger:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
        return self._append(unit, kind, actor, payload)

    def _append(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, payload: dict) -> Event:
        self._seq += 1
        ev = Event(seq=self._seq, unit=unit, kind=kind, actor=actor, payload=dict(payload))
        self._events.append(ev)
        if self._path:
            # One line, one open, closed immediately: the record is with the OS before emit() returns.
            # An event that is in memory but not on disk is a history that disagrees with itself the
            # moment the process dies.
            with open(self._path, 'a') as fh:
                fh.write(ev.to_json() + '\n')
                fh.flush()
        return ev

content.text:
Root cause is clear: `Ledger._append` allocates seq from an in-memory counter with no cross-process lock, so my `corpus_fetch` run and a concurrent `corpus_test` writer (another wheel) both appended starting at seq 5715 — duplicate seqs. I'll (1) repair the shared ledger, (2) isolate my runner onto its own ledger so it can never corrupt or be corrupted by the shared one, keeping blobs in the shared content-addressed store.

First, repair the shared ledger by re-sequencing in file order (no event lost).

import json
src='outputs/event_ledger.jsonl'
lines=open(src).read().splitlines()
seen=set(); out=[]; dropped=0
for l in lines:
    l=l.strip()
    if not l: continue
    d=json.loads(l)
    # identity = everything but seq (an append-only log merged by union, never by seq)
    ident=json.dumps([d.get('unit'),d.get('kind'),d.get('actor'),round(float(d.get('ts') or 0),6),d.get('payload')],sort_keys=True)
    if ident in seen: dropped+=1; continue
    seen.add(ident); out.append(d)
# stable: preserve original arrival order, then renumber seq 1..N
for i,d in enumerate(out,1): d['seq']=i
with open(src,'w') as f:
    for d in out: f.write(json.dumps({'seq':d['seq'],'unit':d['unit'],'kind':d['kind'],'actor':d['actor'],'payload':d['payload'],'ts':d['ts']},sort_keys=True)+'\n')
print('repaired: kept',len(out),'events, dropped',dropped,'exact-duplicate lines')

input.new_string:
def merge_into_shared() -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Fold our dedicated ledger into the shared one by APPEND-ONLY UNION, under an flock.

    NEVER rewrites the shared file — it reads it fresh (for the max seq and the identities already
    present), then APPENDS only the events it does not already carry, with continuing seq numbers. A
    concurrent, non-cooperating appender can at worst cause a rare seq collision (which the loader flags
    and a re-sequence fixes) — it can NEVER lose an event, which a truncate-and-rewrite merge would.
    -> (n_appended, n_already_present).
    """
    if not CORPUS_LEDGER.exists():
        return 0, 0
    mine = [json.loads(l) for l in CORPUS_LEDGER.read_text().splitlines() if l.strip()]
    lock_path = SHARED_LEDGER.with_suffix('.merge.lock')
    with open(lock_path, 'w') as lock:
        fcntl.flock(lock, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
        present, max_seq = set(), 0
        if SHARED_LEDGER.exists():
            for l in SHARED_LEDGER.read_text().splitlines():
                l = l.strip()
                if not l:
                    continue
                d = json.loads(l)
                max_seq = max(max_seq, int(d.get('seq', 0)))
                present.add(json.dumps([d.get('unit'), d.get('kind'), d.get('actor'),
                                        round(float(d.get('ts') or 0), 6), d.get('payload')],
                                       sort_keys=True))
        appended = 0
        with open(SHARED_LEDGER, 'a') as fh:
            for d in mine:
                ident = json.dumps([d.get('unit'), d.get('kind'), d.get('actor'),
                                    round(float(d.get('ts') or 0), 6), d.get('payload')],
                                   sort_keys=True)
                if ident in present:
                    continue
                max_seq += 1
                fh.write(json.dumps({'seq': max_seq, 'unit': d['unit'], 'kind': d['kind'],
                                     'actor': d['actor'], 'payload': d['payload'], 'ts': d['ts']},
                                    sort_keys=True) + '\n')
                present.add(ident)
                appended += 1
            fh.flush()
            os.fsync(fh.fileno())
        fcntl.flock(lock, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
    return appended, len(mine) - appended


def write_status(done: dict, total: int, i: int) -> None:

content.text: The scheduler is thread-safe: `_Locked` opens a fresh fd and flocks per call, so threads serialize per-host and run parallel across hosts — exactly what we want. I'll give each worker thread its own Acquirer+ledger file (single-writer, no lock needed), share the content-addressed BlobStore and the flocked scheduler, and merge all worker ledgers into the shared one under flock. Let me stop the serial run and refactor.

CORPUS_LEDGER = CAMPAIGN / 'corpus_fetch_ledger.jsonl'
SHARED_LEDGER = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'event_ledger.jsonl'
#: Every file this run writes ledger events to (the serial-run ledger + one per worker thread). ALL of
#: them are folded into the shared ledger by the flock append-only union — the ONLY writer to the shared
#: ledger, ever.
LEDGER_GLOB = 'corpus_fetch_ledger*.jsonl'

#: In-process worker pool size. 1,005 DOIs span ~292 registrant prefixes; the busiest host is ~10% of
#: them, so a serial loop leaves ~90% of hosts idle waiting on one. Workers keep many hosts busy at once

def write_status(done: dict, total: int, i: int) -> None:
    hist: dict = {}
    for rec in done.values():
        hist[rec['outcome']] = hist.get(rec['outcome'], 0) + 1
    STATUS.write_text(json.dumps({
        'ts': time.time(), 'processed': len(done), 'queue_total': total,
        'position': i, 'histogram': dict(sorted(hist.items(), key=lambda kv: -kv[1])),
    }, indent=1))


# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument('--limit', type=int, default=0, help='process only the first N pending DOIs')
    ap.add_argument('--offset', type=int, default=0)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    queue = [norm_doi(d) for d in json.loads(QUEUE.read_text())]
    seen, ordered = set(), []
    for d in queue:
        if d and d not in seen:
            seen.add(d)
            ordered.append(d)
    total = len(ordered)

    cache = load_resolved_cache()
    done = load_checkpoint()

    ledger = open_ledger(CORPUS_LEDGER)         # OUR OWN ledger — never collides with other wheels
    blobs = BlobStore()                         # THE immutable, content-addressed store (outputs/blobs)
    acq = Acquirer('corpus_fetch', ledger=ledger, blobs=blobs)

    print(f'=== corpus fetch: {total} unique DOIs | {len(cache)} pre-resolved in cache | '
          f'{len(done)} already on the checkpoint ===', flush=True)
    print(f'    blob store: {blobs.root}', flush=True)
    print(f'    ledger:     {ledger._path}  ({len(ledger)} events)\n', flush=True)

    # ── PASSES. One pass processes every pending DOI once. RETRIABLE outcomes (OpenAlex deferred by our
    #    own governor, a 429, a hang) are facts about OUR request rate, not the world — so we do more
    #    passes: between them the per-host `not_before` expires and the breaker clears, and a DOI that
    #    DEFERRED because OpenAlex was cooling down resolves cleanly next time. We stop when nothing is
    #    pending, or a whole pass converted ZERO retriables to terminal (no progress => waiting longer
    #    will not help this process), or MAX_PASSES is hit. `--limit` forces a single pass (test slice).
    max_passes = 1 if args.limit else int(os.environ.get('POLARIS_MAX_PASSES', '6'))
    for p in range(1, max_passes + 1):
        pending = [d for d in ordered
                   if done.get(d, {}).get('outcome') not in TERMINAL]   # retriable + unstarted
        if args.offset:
            pending = pending[args.offset:]
        if args.limit:
            pending = pending[:args.limit]
        if not pending:
            print(f'\n=== all DOIs terminal after pass {p - 1}. nothing retriable remains. ===',
                  flush=True)
            break
        n_terminal_before = sum(1 for r in done.values() if r.get('outcome') in TERMINAL)
        print(f'\n=== PASS {p}/{max_passes}: {len(pending)} DOIs pending '
              f'(terminal skipped; retriable re-attempted) ===\n', flush=True)

        for i, doi in enumerate(pending, 1):
            entry = cache.get(doi)
            try:
                rec = process_doi(acq, ledger, blobs, doi, entry)
            except Exception as e:
                rec = {'doi': doi, 'outcome': 'BACKEND_FAILED',
                       'detail': {'error': f'{type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:200]}'}, 'ts': time.time()}
            append_checkpoint(rec)
            done[norm_doi(doi)] = rec
            d = rec['detail']
            print(f"  p{p} [{i:>4}/{len(pending)}] {rec['outcome']:<14} "
                  f"loc={d.get('n_locations', '?'):>2} got={d.get('n_fetched', 0)} "
                  f"cls={d.get('content_class', '')} expr={d.get('expression_kind', '')} "
                  f"words={d.get('readable_words', 0)} :: {doi}", flush=True)
            if i % 10 == 0 or i == len(pending):
                write_status(done, total, i)

        n_terminal_after = sum(1 for r in done.values() if r.get('outcome') in TERMINAL)
        gained = n_terminal_after - n_terminal_before
        try:
            app, dup = merge_into_shared()
            print(f'\n=== PASS {p} done: +{gained} terminal this pass | merged {app} new events '
                  f'into shared ledger ({dup} already present) ===', flush=True)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f'\n=== PASS {p} done: +{gained} terminal this pass | MERGE DEFERRED '
                  f'({type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:120]}) — corpus ledger is durable, merge later ===',
                  flush=True)
        if gained == 0:
            print('    no retriable DOI converted this pass — waiting longer will not help. Stopping.',
                  flush=True)
            break
        if p < max_passes:
            # let per-host not_before windows expire before hammering the governor again
            time.sleep(float(os.environ.get('POLARIS_PASS_COOLDOWN_S', '30')))

    write_status(done, total, total)
    try:
        app, dup = merge_into_shared()
        print(f'\n=== FINAL MERGE: {app} new events appended to shared ledger '
              f'({dup} already present) ===', flush=True)
    except Exception as e:

content.text: Now the core refactor — thread-local per-worker Acquirer/ledger, shared blobs, one executor across passes, locked checkpoint/done. First the setup and startup print:

input.old_string:
ledger = open_ledger(CORPUS_LEDGER)         # OUR OWN ledger — never collides with other wheels

    acq = Acquirer('corpus_fetch', ledger=ledger, blobs=blobs)

input.new_string:
# ── ONE shared, content-addressed blob store (collision-free by construction — same bytes => same
    #    path, so concurrent workers writing the same document is idempotent). ──────────────────────
    blobs = BlobStore()

    # ── EACH WORKER THREAD GETS ITS OWN Acquirer + its OWN ledger FILE. A per-thread ledger is
    #    SINGLE-WRITER, so it needs no lock and can never collide; the shared ledger is touched ONLY by
    #    the flock append-only union in merge_into_shared(). Every network request still goes through the
    #    module-level host SCHEDULER, whose per-host flock serialises same-host workers and lets
    #    different-host workers run at once — so politeness is set by the token bucket, not the pool. ──
    _tl = threading.local()

    def worker_acq() -> tuple[Acquirer, object]:
        if not hasattr(_tl, 'acq'):
            lp = CAMPAIGN / f'corpus_fetch_ledger.t{threading.get_ident()}.jsonl'
            led = open_ledger(lp)
            _tl.acq = Acquirer('corpus_fetch', ledger=led, blobs=blobs)
            _tl.ledger = led
        return _tl.acq, _tl.ledger

    lock = threading.Lock()                      # guards `done`, the checkpoint file, and the counter

    print(f'=== corpus fetch: {total} unique DOIs | {len(cache)} pre-resolved in cache | '
          f'{len(done)} already on the checkpoint ===', flush=True)
    print(f'    blob store: {blobs.root}', flush=True)
    print(f'    worker pool: {WORKERS} threads  (POLARIS_FETCH_WORKERS)  '
          f'| per-host politeness via host_scheduler token bucket', flush=True)
    print(f'    ledgers:    {CAMPAIGN}/{LEDGER_GLOB}  -> flock-merged into {SHARED_LEDGER}\n',
          flush=True)

max_passes = 1 if args.limit else int(os.environ.get('POLARIS_MAX_PASSES', '6'))

    def handle(job) -> None:
        """Process ONE DOI on a worker thread. The only shared state it touches — `done`, the checkpoint
        file, the progress counter — is written under `lock`; the fetch itself is fully independent."""
        p, doi, n_pending, counter = job
        entry = cache.get(doi)
        acqw, ledw = worker_acq()
        try:
            rec = process_doi(acqw, ledw, blobs, doi, entry)
        except Exception as e:
            rec = {'doi': doi, 'outcome': 'BACKEND_FAILED',
                   'detail': {'error': f'{type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:200]}'}, 'ts': time.time()}
        with lock:
            counter[0] += 1
            i = counter[0]
            append_checkpoint(rec)
            done[norm_doi(doi)] = rec
            d = rec['detail']
            print(f"  p{p} [{i:>4}/{n_pending}] {rec['outcome']:<14} "
                  f"loc={d.get('n_locations', '?'):>2} got={d.get('n_fetched', 0)} "
                  f"cls={d.get('content_class', '')} expr={d.get('expression_kind', '')} "
                  f"words={d.get('readable_words', 0)} :: {doi}", flush=True)
            if i % 20 == 0 or i == n_pending:
                write_status(done, total, i)

    # ONE executor across all passes: worker threads (and therefore their per-thread ledger files) are
    # reused, so the ledger-file count stays bounded at WORKERS no matter how many passes run.
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=WORKERS) as ex:
        for p in range(1, max_passes + 1):
            pending = [d for d in ordered
                       if done.get(d, {}).get('outcome') not in TERMINAL]   # retriable + unstarted
            if args.offset:
                pending = pending[args.offset:]
            if args.limit:
                pending = pending[:args.limit]
            if not pending:
                print(f'\n=== all DOIs terminal after pass {p - 1}. nothing retriable remains. ===',
                      flush=True)
                break
            n_terminal_before = sum(1 for r in done.values() if r.get('outcome') in TERMINAL)
            print(f'\n=== PASS {p}/{max_passes}: {len(pending)} DOIs pending, {WORKERS} workers '
                  f'(terminal skipped; retriable re-attempted) ===\n', flush=True)

            counter = [0]
            jobs = [(p, doi, len(pending), counter) for doi in pending]
            list(ex.map(handle, jobs))           # blocks until the pass is fully drained

            n_terminal_after = sum(1 for r in done.values() if r.get('outcome') in TERMINAL)
            gained = n_terminal_after - n_terminal_before
            try:
                app, dup = merge_into_shared()
                print(f'\n=== PASS {p} done: +{gained} terminal this pass | merged {app} new events '
                      f'into shared ledger ({dup} already present) ===', flush=True)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f'\n=== PASS {p} done: +{gained} terminal this pass | MERGE DEFERRED '
                      f'({type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:120]}) — worker ledgers are durable, merge later '
                      f'===', flush=True)
            if gained == 0:
                print('    no retriable DOI converted this pass — waiting longer will not help. '
                      'Stopping.', flush=True)
                break
            if p < max_passes:
                # let per-host not_before windows expire before hammering the governor again
                time.sleep(float(os.environ.get('POLARIS_PASS_COOLDOWN_S', '30')))

content.text:
The parallel run is healthy and the coordinator's request is fully addressed. Everything is verified. Here's my report.

## Report: corpus fetch runner — built, hardened, parallelized, and running

### What was built
A resumable orchestrator over the existing verified infrastructure at `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/corpus_fetch.py`. It does NOT reimplement fetching — it drives `acquisition.Acquirer.get` (the one network door, through the file-locked host scheduler), `deep_fetch.fetch_text` (records manifestations), the `resolve_locations` helpers, and the `event_ledger` reducers. Every per-DOI outcome is DERIVED by the reducers, never asserted:
- content: `FETCHED | ABSTRACT_ONLY | LANDING_PAGE | WRONG_WORK | UNREADABLE | CITATION_ONLY`
- transport: `ACCESS_DENIED | THROTTLED | DEFERRED | BACKEND_FAILED | NOT_FOUND | NO_LOCATION | RESOLVE_FAILED`

Fetch order honors Sol §3: PMC JATS (via Europe PMC `fullTextXML`) / publisher OA VoR > repository VoR > preprint/AM as last-resort leads (never relabelled a journal version — `expression_kind` keeps them honest). Stops early on a complete full-text; one logical request per publisher URL; resolves live via OpenAlex+Unpaywall for the 705 queue DOIs not in `resolved_locations.json`.

### Test-8 proof (with real blobs)
First run: 2 clean `FETCHED` (real full text 1508 & 5033 words, `journal_version`). Blobs grew 68→76 on disk in `outputs/blobs` (content-addressed). A verified PMC blob: `outputs/blobs/ba/baa91d9…bin` = 225,606 bytes decoding to the correct article title matching its DOI.

### Two real bugs found and fixed
1. **Silent-drop guard bug (root-caused).** `event_ledger.observe_text` emits `article_front_matter`/`cover_sheet_text` (raw document text), but those were missing from the guard's `VERBATIM_KEYS` world-text exemption. Any real article whose front matter contains a reserved word (e.g. a PubMed page's "Complete list of authors") raised `ForbiddenLabel` and lost the fetch. Two JAMA/PMC articles hit this. Fix: added both keys to `VERBATIM_KEYS` in `scripts/event_ledger.py` (one-line, matches the guard's own documented intent — raw world text is exempt from the value scan). `event_ledger.py` self-test passes. On rerun those 2 DOIs went `BACKEND_FAILED → FETCHED` (3685 & 5140 words), also proving resumability.
2. **Shared-ledger corruption from concurrent writers.** `Ledger._append` allocates seq from an in-memory counter with no cross-process lock, so my run and a concurrent `corpus_test` writer both appended from seq 5715 → duplicate seqs → `LedgerCorrupt`. I repaired `outputs/event_ledger.jsonl` by dedup-and-re-sequence (kept all 6011 events, backup at `outputs/event_ledger.jsonl.bak_precorpus`), then **isolated the runner**: each worker writes its own single-writer ledger file, and the shared ledger is touched ONLY by an flock'd, append-only union (`merge_into_shared` — never rewrites, so a concurrent appender can never be clobbered).

### Parallelization (coordinator request — done, invariants held)
Replaced the serial loop with a `ThreadPoolExecutor` (default 10, `POLARIS_FETCH_WORKERS`). Safety invariants verified intact:
- Every request still goes through `host_scheduler.acquire` — its `_Locked` opens a fresh fd + flock per call, so same-host workers serialize and different-host workers run concurrently. Politeness is set by the token bucket, not the pool.
- All shared-ledger writes still go through the flock append-only union; each worker's own ledger is single-writer (no lock needed).
- Blob content-hash idempotency preserved; `done`/checkpoint/counter guarded by a threading.Lock.
- Per-DOI classification and retriable-across-restart semantics unchanged. Auto-loops up to 6 passes to recover governor-deferred retriables, stopping when a pass converts zero.

### Launched and measured
Detached run alive (PID 196099, 10 worker threads). **Blob-growth rate: 43 blobs/min and 64 DOIs/min — ~3× the serial baseline** (~15-19 blobs/min, ~23 DOIs/min). Not 10× because polite per-host buckets + the shared OpenAlex resolver (1.0s spacing) cap same-host throughput; parallelism pays off across the 296 pre-resolved DOIs fanning out over many publisher/PMC hosts.

Live histogram (186 unique DOIs so far): **FETCHED 52** (real journal full text), NOT_FOUND 18, ACCESS_DENIED 14 (publisher 403s, honest), LANDING_PAGE 7, ABSTRACT_ONLY 2, WRONG_WORK 1, UNREADABLE 1, DEFERRED 4 + RESOLVE_FAILED 87 (retriable — OpenAlex governor deferrals plus ~72 genuinely malformed SICI DOIs in the queue; later passes recover the deferrable ones). Shared ledger loads clean (6601 events).

Launch command: `setsid nohup python /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/corpus_fetch.py > .../corpus_fetch.log 2>&1 < /dev/null &`

Nothing committed. Did not touch report_ast/synthesis_contract/argument_planner/cohesion. The one shared-infra change (`event_ledger.py` `VERBATIM_KEYS`) is flagged above for review — it was necessary to stop real articles being silently dropped.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CORPUS TRUTH — does every label on this corpus actually describe its content?

Tonight, every single failure had the same shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN THE CONTENT
SUPPORTED, and nothing checked.

    "gate: WIRED"        -- it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
    "span-verified"      -- verified by its first 60 characters
    "fabrication-proof"  -- the table printed model-written prose
    "still paywalled"    -- we asked by DOI; the free copy is a separate work
    "FULLTEXT"           -- 535 words. An abstract.

Not one of them announced itself. Each read as a fact about the world.

This script re-derives every corpus label FROM THE CONTENT and rewrites it to the truth. It is
idempotent, it never deletes text, and it is allowed to make the corpus look worse -- because a
corpus that looks worse and IS honest is the only kind we can build on.

    python scripts/corpus_truth.py [--fix]
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import collections
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
from acquisition import held_content_class                      # noqa: E402
from event_ledger import C_ABSTRACT, C_FULLTEXT, LEGACY_STATUS  # noqa: E402

CORPUS = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
NUMS = re.compile(r'\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b')

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# THERE WAS A `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` HERE, AND AN `ABSTRACT_MIN = 120`. They are deleted, and this is

40	# THE THIRD ONE. `event_ledger.py` deleted its own `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` (its headstone is still in
41	# that file); `provenance.py`'s registry uses a scholarly floor of 1,200 and NO FLOOR AT ALL for a
42	# judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record, which are complete at any length.
43	# And this file — which `run_ladder.sh` and `post_fetch.sh` BOTH run IMMEDIATELY AFTER the merge, with
44	# `--fix` — held a third number, knew about neither of the others, and OVERWROTE their answer.
45	#
46	# It was not a redundant check. It was a LOUDER one, and it ran LAST:
47	#
48	#     * a 105-word judicial opinion is COMPLETE (registry: stub_floor=None). This file called it
49	#       CITATION_ONLY and then POPPED ITS TEXT.
50	#     * a 2,600-word aeaweb cookie banner clears 2,500. This file called it FULLTEXT.
51	#
52	# Three modules holding three different numbers is not three checks. It is no rule at all: which
53	# document a card could cite depended on which module happened to look at it last, and this one always
54	# did. So completeness is not decided here. It is READ FROM THE ONE REDUCER, over the bytes.
55	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56	
57	
58	def truth(c: dict) -> str:
59	    """The label the CONTENT earns -- not the one it was given, and not one this file invented.
60	
61	    Delegates to `event_ledger.derive_content_profile` (through `acquisition.held_content_class`),
62	    which is the SAME reducer `merge_corpus` and the fetchers' target selectors use, driven by the
63	    SAME registry in `provenance.KIND_PROFILE`. There is now exactly one answer in the codebase to
64	    "is this document complete", and this file no longer has a second opinion.
65	    """
66	    cls, _info = held_content_class(c)
67	    if cls == 'CITATION_ONLY' and len((c.get('abstract') or '').split()) >= 120:
68	        # We hold no document, but we DO hold the bibliography's abstract. That is real, quotable text
69	        # — it is simply not the paper. (The floor is the registry's SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR.)
70	        return 'ABSTRACT_ONLY'
71	    return LEGACY_STATUS.get(cls, 'CITATION_ONLY')
72	
73	
74	def main() -> int:
75	    fix = '--fix' in sys.argv
76	    rows = json.loads(CORPUS.read_text())
77	
78	    lied = []
79	    for c in rows:
80	        cls, info = held_content_class(c)
81	        was, is_ = c.get('content_status'), truth(c)
82	        if was != is_:
83	            lied.append((c.get('authors', ['?'])[0], c.get('year'),
84	                         len((c.get('fulltext') or '').split()), was, is_))
85	        if fix:
86	            # ── A DOCUMENT THAT IS NOT A PAPER MUST NOT BE READ AS ONE — UNDER ANY FIELD NAME. ────
87	            #
88	            # This block used to say:
89	            #
90	            #     if is_ != 'FULLTEXT' and c.get('fulltext'):
91	            #         c['abstract'] = c.get('abstract') or c['fulltext'][:8000]   # <-- HERE
92	            #         c.pop('fulltext', None)
93	            #
94	            # which MOVED THE COOKIE BANNER INTO THE `abstract` FIELD. On this pass the row is
95	            # CITATION_ONLY and the miner skips it, so it looks harmless. IT IS NOT, BECAUSE THIS
96	            # SCRIPT IS IDEMPOTENT AND GETS RUN AGAIN: on the second run the reducer finds no
97	            # `fulltext`, sees 535 words of banner sitting in `abstract`, and promotes the row to
98	            # ABSTRACT_ONLY — which PASSES the miner's `!= 'CITATION_ONLY'` test. The banner becomes
99	            # evidence, and it laundered itself through the FIX, in two hops, using nothing but this
100	            # script's own idempotency.
101	            #
102	            # So bytes that are not a document go to a field NOTHING READS. They are not deleted —
103	            # nothing here is ever deleted, and merge_corpus has them blobbed and hashed besides.
104	            if cls != C_FULLTEXT and (c.get('fulltext') or '').strip():
105	                if cls == C_ABSTRACT and not (c.get('abstract') or '').strip():
106	                    c['abstract'] = c['fulltext'][:8000]     # a GENUINE scholarly fragment. It is one.
107	                else:
108	                    c['withheld_text'] = c['fulltext']       # a banner / a glyph dump / a stranger
109	                    c['withheld_because'] = str(info.get('reason', ''))[:200]
110	                c.pop('fulltext', None)
111	                c['fulltext_words'] = 0
112	            c['content_status'] = is_
113	            c['content_class'] = cls
114	            c['content_status_derived_by'] = 'event_ledger.derive_content_profile'
115	
116	    print(f'=== CORPUS TRUTH: {len(rows)} papers ===\n')
117	    if lied:
118	        print(f'  {len(lied)} LABEL(S) THAT DID NOT DESCRIBE THEIR CONTENT:')
119	        for au, yr, w, was, is_ in lied[:14]:
120	            print(f'    {au[:16]:<16} {yr}  {w:>6,}w   {was:<14} -> {is_}')
121	    else:
122	        print('  every label is earned by its content.')
123	
124	    if fix:
125	        CORPUS.write_text(json.dumps(rows, indent=1))
126	        print(f'\n  rewritten to the truth.')
127	
128	    st = collections.Counter(truth(c) for c in rows)
129	    real = sum(len(NUMS.findall(c.get('fulltext') or '')) for c in rows if truth(c) == 'FULLTEXT')
130	    print(f'\n  FULLTEXT      : {st["FULLTEXT"]}')
131	    print(f'  ABSTRACT_ONLY : {st["ABSTRACT_ONLY"]}')
132	    print(f'  CITATION_ONLY : {st["CITATION_ONLY"]}')
133	    print(f'  USABLE AS EVIDENCE: {st["FULLTEXT"] + st["ABSTRACT_ONLY"]}/{len(rows)}')
134	    print(f'\n  QUANTITATIVE CLAIMS IN REAL FULL TEXT: {real:,}')
135	    print(f'  (the report printed 2. cellcog prints 202. the extractor read only the first 31.9%'
136	          f' of each paper -- the introductions.)')
137	    return 1 if (lied and not fix) else 0
138	
139	
140	if __name__ == '__main__':

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""MERGE CORPUS — union of EVENT STREAMS and IMMUTABLE MANIFESTATIONS. There is no winner.
3	
4	WHAT THIS FILE USED TO BE
5	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6	Two fetchers loaded one JSON file, both improved it, and both wrote it back whole — so the last writer
7	silently flattened the other's work. This script was the patch for that, and it merged by DOI keeping,
8	for each paper, THE VERSION WITH THE MOST TEXT:
9	
10	    RANK = {'FULLTEXT': 3, 'ABSTRACT_ONLY': 2, 'CITATION_ONLY': 1, None: 0}
11	    if (textlen(c), RANK.get(c['content_status'])) > (textlen(cur), RANK.get(cur['content_status'])):
12	        best[k] = c                                            # ...and the loser was DROPPED
13	
14	Both of those keys are the disease.
15	
16	  TEXT LENGTH does not distinguish a paper from a cookie banner. The aeaweb.org landing page for
17	  Autor (2015) is 535 words; the ORA landing page for Frey & Osborne is 548. Either one BEATS a real
18	  400-word abstract on length, and this line would hand it the row.
19	
20	  CLAIMED STATUS is the label the FAILING FETCHER WROTE ABOUT ITSELF. Ranking by it means asking the
21	  component whether it succeeded and believing the answer — which is how `FULLTEXT` came to sit on
22	  535 words of cookie banner in the first place. The merge did not just fail to catch that lie; it
23	  USED IT AS THE TIEBREAK.
24	
25	  And `best[k] = c` DELETED the loser. Two fetchers that retrieved two DIFFERENT DOCUMENTS for one
26	  DOI — which is exactly what happened, an NBER working paper and a publisher landing page — had one
27	  of them thrown away, unrecorded, on the strength of a word count.
28	
29	WHAT IT IS NOW
30	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	An append-only log admits exactly one merge: UNION. A content-addressed blob admits exactly one
32	merge: UNION. So:
33	
34	  1. EVENT STREAMS are unioned and re-sequenced. An observation is not a slot to be overwritten.
35	  2. Legacy bytes sitting on corpus rows are INGESTED as manifestations — hashed, blobbed, profiled.
36	     NOTHING IS DELETED. If two files hold two different documents for one DOI, we now hold BOTH,
37	     forever, and the graph carries both.
38	  3. The row's `fulltext` is a DERIVED VIEW over the manifestations, chosen by the SHARED REDUCER —
39	     the complete, finding-bearing document, and among those the most AUTHORITATIVE VERSION (peer
40	     review changes numbers). Length breaks ties only between documents already agreed to be the same
41	     kind of thing. Nothing is discarded to build that view; it is a pointer, and it is re-derivable.
42	
43	Usage: python scripts/merge_corpus.py <base.json> <other.json> [<other2.json> ...]
44	"""
45	from __future__ import annotations
46	
47	import json
48	import sys
49	from pathlib import Path
50	
51	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
52	
53	from acquisition import (  # noqa: E402
54	    LEDGER_PATH, Acquirer, BlobStore, classify_manifestation, manifestations_of, merge_ledgers,
55	    open_ledger, select_evidence,
56	)
57	from event_ledger import (  # noqa: E402
58	    C_ABSTRACT, C_FULLTEXT, LEGACY_STATUS, derive_route_status, derive_semantic_binding,
59	    record_content_profile,
60	)
61	
62	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
63	CORPUS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
64	
65	
66	def unit_of(row: dict) -> str:
67	    return row.get('doi') or (row.get('title') or '')[:80]
68	
69	
70	def ingest_legacy_bytes(acq: Acquirer, ledger, row: dict, source_file: str) -> int:
71	    """PRESERVE THE ACQUIRED BYTES. Text that is on a corpus row but has no manifestation behind it is
72	    still text WE HOLD — it just has no provenance yet. Give it one; never drop it.
73	
74	    This is the step that makes the union safe. Without it, "merge by manifestation" would silently
75	    discard every byte fetched before the ledger existed — which would be this script's original sin
76	    (deleting the loser) committed again, at ten times the scale, in the fix for it.
77	    """
78	    unit = unit_of(row)
79	    have = {m.get('text_sha256') for m in manifestations_of(ledger, unit)}
80	    n = 0
81	    #: WHICH COLUMN the bytes came from. Note the ledger vocabulary: `body`, never `fulltext`.
82	    #: The conclusion guard REFUSED `text_field='fulltext'` — and it was right to. "Full text" is a
83	    #: VERDICT about completeness, and it is the one the reducer exists to reach; a component that
84	    #: writes the word, even as a column name, has stated the conclusion in the log. `body` says the
85	    #: same true thing (these bytes are the document body) and asserts nothing about whether they are
86	    #: all of it. The graph's own `text_field` vocabulary is unchanged — provenance_construct maps it.
87	    for field, column in (('body', 'fulltext'), ('abstract', 'abstract')):
88	        text = (row.get(column) or '').strip()
89	        if not text:
90	            continue
91	        import hashlib
92	        h = hashlib.sha256(text.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
93	        if h in have:
94	            continue                       # content-addressed: we already hold exactly these bytes
95	        acq.record_manifestation(
96	            unit, locator=row.get('oa_url') or '', raw=text.encode('utf-8'), text=text,
97	            adapter=f'corpus:{source_file}',
98	            requested_title=row.get('title') or '',
99	            requested_authors=list(row.get('authors') or []),
100	            requested_doi=row.get('doi') or '',
101	            requested_venue=row.get('venue') or '',
102	            requested_year=row.get('year'),
103	            source_type=str(row.get('type') or 'journal-article'),
104	            extraction_method='corpus_ingest', source_field=field,
105	            # The locator is the row's `oa_url`, which for the rows wp_fetch touched points at the
106	            # PUBLISHER while the bytes are an NBER working paper. We record it and let the graph
107	            # contradict it in the open, rather than adopting it silently.
108	            locator_recorded_by='corpus_row_not_fetcher')
109	        have.add(h)
110	        n += 1
111	    return n
112	
113	
114	def main() -> int:
115	    paths = [Path(p) for p in sys.argv[1:] if not p.startswith('-')]
116	    if len(paths) < 2:
117	        print(__doc__)
118	        return 2
119	
120	    ledger_path = Path(LEDGER_PATH)
121	
122	    # ── 1. UNION THE EVENT STREAMS. ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
123	    # A ledger beside each input (`<corpus>.events.jsonl`) is merged in too, so a fetcher that ran
124	    # against its own log does not lose its history to the one that ran against the main log.
125	    side = [p.with_suffix('.events.jsonl') for p in paths]
126	    n_ev, dupes = merge_ledgers([ledger_path] + [s for s in side if s.exists()], ledger_path)
127	    print(f'  event streams merged  : {n_ev:,} events ({dupes} duplicate observations dropped)')
128	
129	    ledger = open_ledger(ledger_path)
130	    blobs = BlobStore()
131	    acq = Acquirer('merge_corpus', ledger=ledger, blobs=blobs)
132	
133	    # ── 2. UNION THE ROWS, AND INGEST EVERY BYTE ANY OF THEM CARRIES. ─────────────────────────────
134	    rows: dict[str, dict] = {}
135	    ingested = 0
136	    for p in paths:
137	        if not p.exists():
138	            print(f'  skip (missing): {p}')
139	            continue
140	        data = json.loads(p.read_text())
141	        for c in data:
142	            k = unit_of(c)
143	            ingested += ingest_legacy_bytes(acq, ledger, c, p.name)
144	            if k not in rows:
145	                rows[k] = dict(c)
146	            else:
147	                # Merge the row's METADATA (citations, venue, authors...). The TEXT is not merged here
148	                # and is not fought over here: it lives in the manifestations, and the view is derived
149	                # below. There is nothing left for the last writer to win.
150	                for kk, vv in c.items():
151	                    if kk in ('fulltext', 'abstract', 'content_status', 'fulltext_source',
152	                              'fulltext_words', 'oa_url'):
153	                        continue
154	                    if vv not in (None, '', [], {}) and not rows[k].get(kk):
155	                        rows[k][kk] = vv
156	        print(f'  {p.name:<44} {len(data):>3} rows')
157	    print(f'  legacy bytes ingested : {ingested} new manifestation(s) — nothing was dropped')
158	
159	    # ── 3. DERIVE THE VIEW. NO WINNER IS CHOSEN BY LENGTH, AND NO LOSER IS DELETED. ───────────────
160	    out = []
161	    stats: dict[str, int] = {}
162	    multi = 0
163	    for k, row in rows.items():
164	        unit = unit_of(row)
165	        manifs = manifestations_of(ledger, unit)
166	        if len(manifs) > 1:
167	            multi += 1
168	
169	        # every set of bytes we hold for this work, kept, with what the shared reducer makes of it
170	        row['manifestations'] = [
171	            dict(text_sha256=c['manifestation'].get('text_sha256', ''),
172	                 blob_id=c['manifestation'].get('blob_id', ''),
173	                 locator=c['manifestation'].get('locator', ''),
174	                 adapter=c['manifestation'].get('adapter', ''),
175	                 n_words=c['readable_words'],
176	                 artifact_kind=c['artifact_kind'],
177	                 expression_kind=c['expression_kind'],
178	                 content_class=c['content_class'])
179	            for c in (classify_manifestation(m, blobs) for m in manifs)]
180	
181	        # THE LABEL, DERIVED. `record_content_profile` runs the reducer over the observations and
182	        # MINUTES the verdict in the ledger as an audit artifact that no reducer will ever read back.
183	        # This script may do that because it IS a reducer. A fetcher may not, and now cannot: there is
184	        # no API in `acquisition` that takes a status.
185	        cls, info = record_content_profile(ledger, unit)
186	        binding, _ = derive_semantic_binding(ledger.events(unit))
187	        route = derive_route_status(ledger.events(unit))
188	        best = select_evidence(ledger, unit, blobs)
189	
190	        # ---- THE ROW IS A VIEW. Only bytes the reducer calls a DOCUMENT are presented as one. ------
191	        if cls == C_FULLTEXT and best is not None:
192	            row['fulltext'] = best['text'][:120000]
193	            row['fulltext_words'] = len(row['fulltext'].split())
194	            row['oa_url'] = best['manifestation'].get('locator') or row.get('oa_url') or ''
195	            row['fulltext_manifestation'] = best['manifestation'].get('text_sha256', '')[:12]
196	            row['fulltext_selected_because'] = best['basis'][:200]
197	        else:
198	            # These bytes are NOT the paper. THEY ARE NOT DELETED — they are in the blob store,
199	            # addressed by their own hash, and every one of them is a node in the provenance graph.
200	            # They are simply not handed to the miner under the name `fulltext`, because the miner
201	            # reads that field as "the words of this paper" and they are not.
202	            if (row.get('fulltext') or '').strip():
203	                row['fulltext_withheld_because'] = str(info.get('reason', ''))[:200]
204	                row['fulltext_withheld_manifestation'] = (
205	                    best['manifestation'].get('text_sha256', '')[:12] if best else '')
206	                row.pop('fulltext', None)
207	                row['fulltext_words'] = 0
208	            if cls == C_ABSTRACT and best is not None and not (row.get('abstract') or '').strip():
209	                row['abstract'] = best['text'][:8000]
210	
211	        # `fulltext_source` NAMED THE SCRIPT THAT RAN. It agreed with the truth zero times out of six.
212	        # It is not corrected here — IT IS REMOVED, and replaced by what the bytes' own header says.
213	        row.pop('fulltext_source', None)
214	
215	        # THE COARSE LABEL the miner and composer contract on, PROJECTED from the derived class in the
216	        # one place that knows the mapping — and projected SAFE: a cookie banner is not an abstract.
217	        row['content_status'] = LEGACY_STATUS[cls]
218	        row['content_class'] = cls                        # ...and the precise class is right beside it
219	        row['content_status_basis'] = str(info.get('reason', ''))[:300]
220	        row['content_status_derived_by'] = 'event_ledger.derive_content_profile'
221	        row['artifact_kind'] = info.get('artifact_kind')
222	        row['semantic_binding'] = binding               # SAME_WORK / VERSION_OF_PREPRINT / ...
223	        row['route_state'] = route.state
224	        stats[cls] = stats.get(cls, 0) + 1
225	        out.append(row)
226	
227	    CORPUS.write_text(json.dumps(out, indent=1))
228	
229	    print(f'\n=== MERGED CORPUS: {len(out)} papers ===')
230	    for k in sorted(stats, key=lambda x: -stats[x]):
231	        print(f'    {k:<22}{stats[k]:>4}')
232	    print(f'\n    works holding MORE THAN ONE document : {multi}   <- the old merge deleted one of each')
233	    print(f'    total manifestations retained        : {sum(len(r.get("manifestations") or []) for r in out)}')
234	    print(f'    wrote {CORPUS}')
235	    print(f'    wrote {ledger_path}  ({len(ledger)} events)')
236	    print('\n  Every label above was DERIVED by `event_ledger.derive_content_profile` from the events,')
237	    print('  and every one carries its basis on the row. Not one was ranked by word count, and not')
238	    print('  one was read off a claim a fetcher made about its own success.')
239	    return 0
240	
241	
242	if __name__ == '__main__':
243	    raise SystemExit(main())
244

content.text: `merge_corpus.py` IS the canonical reducer-driven assembler — it reads manifestations from the shared ledger (`manifestations_of`/`select_evidence`), derives `fulltext` only when the reducer says `C_FULLTEXT`, removes the untrustworthy `fulltext_source`, and records `semantic_binding`/`expression_kind` per manifestation. It only emits rows for DOIs present in its input files. So the correct path is: seed rows (doi + metadata) for the newly-fetched DOIs → `merge_corpus` → `corpus_truth --fix`.             year = year or m.get('requested_year')

        'venue': venue,
        'year': year,

import json, sys, time, urllib.parse
sys.path.insert(0, 'scripts'); sys.path.insert(0, 'outputs/acquisition_campaign')
from acquisition import Acquirer, open_ledger
from pathlib import Path

MAILTO = 'aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com'
seed = json.load(open('outputs/acquisition_campaign/campaign_corpus_seed.json'))
dois = [s['doi'] for s in seed]

# dedicated ledger (never the shared one)
led = open_ledger(Path('outputs/acquisition_campaign/corpus_meta_backfill_ledger.jsonl'))
acq = Acquirer('corpus_meta_backfill', ledger=led)

def venue_of(w):
    pl = w.get('primary_location') or {}
    src = pl.get('source') or {}
    return src.get('display_name') or (w.get('host_venue') or {}).get('display_name') or ''

meta = {}   # norm_doi -> {title, authors, venue, year}
BATCH = 40
for i in range(0, len(dois), BATCH):
    chunk = dois[i:i+BATCH]
    filt = 'doi:' + '|'.join(urllib.parse.quote(d, safe='') for d in chunk)
    url = f'https://api.openalex.org/works?filter={filt}&per-page={BATCH}&mailto={urllib.parse.quote(MAILTO)}'
    r, body = acq.get_json('meta_backfill', 'openalex', url, tries=4, timeout=40)
    if not (r.ok and isinstance(body, dict)):
        print(f'  batch {i//BATCH}: {r.outcome} http={r.http_status}')
        continue
    for w in body.get('results', []):
        d = (w.get('doi') or '').lower().replace('https://doi.org/', '')
        if not d:
            continue
        authors = [ (a.get('author') or {}).get('display_name') or ''
                    for a in (w.get('authorships') or []) ]
        meta[d] = {'title': w.get('title') or w.get('display_name') or '',
                   'authors': [a for a in authors if a],
                   'venue': venue_of(w),
                   'year': w.get('publication_year')}
    print(f'  batch {i//BATCH}: {len(body.get("results", []))} works')

# enrich seed: OpenAlex fills gaps; never overwrite a non-empty existing value with empty
filled_t = filled_a = 0
for s in seed:
    m = meta.get(s['doi'])
    if not m:
        continue
    if not s['title'] and m['title']:
        s['title'] = m['title']; filled_t += 1
    if not s['authors'] and m['authors']:
        s['authors'] = m['authors']; filled_a += 1
    if not s['venue'] and m['venue']:
        s['venue'] = m['venue']
    if not s['year'] and m['year']:
        s['year'] = m['year']

json.dump(seed, open('outputs/acquisition_campaign/campaign_corpus_seed.json', 'w'), indent=1)
print('backfill done. titles filled:', filled_t, '| author-lists filled:', filled_a)
print('seed now: with title', sum(1 for s in seed if s['title']),
      '| with authors', sum(1 for s in seed if s['authors']),
      '| with venue', sum(1 for s in seed if s['venue']),
      '| of', len(seed))

import json, sys, time, urllib.parse
sys.path.insert(0, 'scripts')
from acquisition import Acquirer, open_ledger
from pathlib import Path

MAILTO = 'aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com'

print(f'rows needing metadata: {len(todo)} of {len(seed)}', flush=True)

def save():
    json.dump(seed, open(SEED, 'w'), indent=1)

ft = fa = 0
for i, s in enumerate(todo, 1):
    if s.get('_meta_tried') and s.get('title') and s.get('authors'):
        continue
    url = f'https://api.crossref.org/works/{urllib.parse.quote(s["doi"], safe="/()")}?mailto={urllib.parse.quote(MAILTO)}'

        m = body.get('message', {}) or {}
        title = (m.get('title') or [''])[0] if m.get('title') else ''
        authors = [ (a.get('given', '') + ' ' + a.get('family', '')).strip() for a in (m.get('author') or []) ]
        authors = [a for a in authors if a]
        venue = (m.get('container-title') or [''])[0] if m.get('container-title') else ''
        year = None
        for key in ('published', 'published-print', 'published-online', 'issued'):
            dp = (m.get(key) or {}).get('date-parts')
            if dp and dp[0] and dp[0][0]:

        if not s['title'] and title: s['title'] = title; ft += 1
        if not s['authors'] and authors: s['authors'] = authors; fa += 1
        if not s['venue'] and venue: s['venue'] = venue
        if not s['year'] and year: s['year'] = year
        s['_meta_tried'] = True
    if i % 20 == 0:
        save()
        print(f'  {i}/{len(todo)} saved | titles+{ft} authors+{fa}', flush=True)

save()
print(f'DONE. titles+{ft} authors+{fa}', flush=True)
print('seed: title', sum(1 for s in seed if s['title']), '| authors', sum(1 for s in seed if s['authors']),

## Corpus assembly — complete, honest, not committed

### Path used (the codebase's own reducer-driven write path — nothing hand-rolled)
1. **Seed rows** for the 489 DOIs where we now hold bytes → `outputs/acquisition_campaign/campaign_corpus_seed.json` (just `doi` + metadata, the exact input `merge_corpus.py` expects).
2. **`python scripts/merge_corpus.py journal_corpus_content.json campaign_corpus_seed.json`** — the canonical assembler. It unioned the event streams, read every manifestation from the shared ledger (`manifestations_of`/`select_evidence`), and DERIVED each row's `fulltext`/`content_status`/`content_class`/`manifestations`/`semantic_binding`/`route_state` from content. It removed the untrustworthy `fulltext_source` and replaced it with per-manifestation `expression_kind`.
3. **`python scripts/corpus_truth.py --fix`** — re-derived every label from content; idempotent-clean on FULLTEXT (zero demotions), honestly promoted 7 CITATION_ONLY→ABSTRACT_ONLY (rows holding a real ≥120-word abstract).

### Final numbers (requested)
- **Corpus rows: 501** (70 prior ∪ 489 seed, 58 overlapping DOIs updated in place)
- **Rows with real full text (post corpus_truth): 377** — up from 23
- **Honest content_status distribution: FULLTEXT 377 · ABSTRACT_ONLY 42 · CITATION_ONLY 82** (usable as evidence: 419/501)
- Finer `content_class` from merge_corpus before corpus_truth's coarse projection: FULLTEXT 377 · ABSTRACT 35 · CITATION_ONLY 63 · NOT_A_DOCUMENT 24 · UNREADABLE 2. (corpus_truth folds NOT_A_DOCUMENT/UNREADABLE into the CITATION_ONLY *status* since it derives class from `row['fulltext']`, which is withheld for non-documents; the fine class + `withheld_because` basis survive on each row and in the manifestations.)
- **54,635 quantitative claims in real full text** (the old report printed 2).
- 565 manifestations retained; 53 works hold >1 document (none deleted).

### Version-binding honesty (your key constraint) — verified clean
`fulltext_source` is gone; version lives per-manifestation from the bytes' own stamps. Among the 377 FULLTEXT rows the selected-manifestation `expression_kind` is: **journal_version 306 · working_paper 11 · accepted_manuscript 11 · preprint 1 · unknown 48**. **Not one working paper is relabeled a journal version** — e.g. `10.1086/705716` carries `expression_kind=working_paper`, `semantic_binding=VERSION_OF_PREPRINT`; accepted manuscripts carry `VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED`. The fabricated-binding failure is not reintroduced.

### Two things to flag for the miner (downstream, yours)
- **13 FULLTEXT rows carry `semantic_binding=DIFFERENT_WORK`** and **60 `UNRESOLVED_BINDING`**. merge_corpus (by its existing design) presents `fulltext` for any complete document and records the binding as a *separate* derived signal on the row — it does not withhold on a bad binding. So these rows hold real full text whose identity is wrong/unproven, honestly flagged. The miner must gate on `semantic_binding` (admit SAME_WORK / version-of-published; quarantine DIFFERENT_WORK; treat VERSION_OF_PREPRINT/ACCEPTED as leads). I did not silently drop them — the honest verdict is on each row for you to enforce.

### One deviation to disclose
The live-resolved DOIs had no bibliographic metadata in the ledger (the resolver stored only OA *locations*), so 143 FETCHED rows lacked titles. OpenAlex's long-term rate budget was exhausted for ~5h by the fetch run, so I backfilled title/authors/venue/year via **Crossref** (`api.crossref.org`, through the one network door, polite) — the other source you named, deterministic, no LLM. Result: 489/489 titles, 481 authors, 483 venues, 488 years.

### Integrity
Shared ledger re-sequenced by merge_corpus and loads clean (23,276 events, 0 duplicates dropped); 1,004 blobs on disk. Backups: `outputs/journal_corpus_content.json.bak_premerge`, `outputs/event_ledger.jsonl.bak_premerge`. **Nothing committed.**
